methodology: Squire taxonomy (declarative/non-declarative) + Baddeley working memory + CoALA (arXiv:2309.02427) agent-memory mapping

systems.name: Working memory
systems.description: Short-lived, capacity-limited active store. CogniFold models this as the immediate hierarchical context band that keeps recently activated nodes salient during scoring.

systems.name: Episodic memory
systems.description: Memory for specific events bound to time and context. CogniFold stores these as EVENT (e-) nodes, the hippocampal/episodic substrate of the graph.

systems.name: Semantic memory
systems.description: General world knowledge abstracted away from individual episodes. CogniFold consolidates recurring EVENTs into CONCEPT (c-) nodes, the neocortical/semantic layer.

systems.name: Prospective / intent memory
systems.cognifold_mechanism: INTENT nodes — src/cognifold/intent/ (UNIQUE: emerges from topology)
systems.description: Remembering to act on goals in the future. CogniFold's INTENT (i-) nodes are a differentiator: proactive goals emerge from graph topology rather than being stored verbatim.

systems.name: Temporal memory
systems.description: Ordering and dating of memories. CogniFold extracts and resolves temporal references into TIME (t-) nodes, anchoring events on a timeline.

systems.name: Systems consolidation
systems.description: Gradual transfer of episodic detail into stable semantic structure. CogniFold consolidates EVENTs into CONCEPTs offline; the schedule and replay dynamics are still maturing.

systems.name: Forgetting / pruning
systems.description: Adaptive removal of low-value memories. CogniFold applies recency decay and prunes orphaned nodes; richer interference-based forgetting is not yet modeled.

systems.name: Procedural memory
systems.description: Skills and habits acquired through practice. Not yet modeled; a candidate for learned action policies over the graph.

systems.name: Priming
systems.description: Implicit facilitation of recognition by prior exposure. Not yet modeled as a distinct activation-history mechanism.

systems.name: Classical conditioning
systems.description: Learned stimulus-response associations. Not yet modeled in the graph.

systems.name: Affective / emotional memory
systems.description: Emotional salience that modulates what is remembered. Not yet modeled; a candidate for affective weighting of node importance.

systems.name: Sensory register
systems.description: Very brief, high-fidelity buffer for raw perceptual input. Out of scope for a text-centric memory graph today.

circuits.cognifold: EVENT layer — verbatim episodic capture; fast one-shot writes preserve what-happened-when.
CONCEPT layer — slow generalization; repeated EVENTs fold into grounded semantic structure.
INTENT layer + hierarchical context window — goals crystallize forward; the working set is assembled per event.
TIME anchors — deadlines and schedules indexed; recency and urgency reshape salience.
Inter-session consolidation — similar CONCEPTs merge and grounded structure is reinforced offline.
Forgetting — recency decay lowers stale salience; orphan pruning removes unsupported nodes.
Procedure nodes (planned) — reusable skills and action sequences, distinct from declarative recall.
Affective salience tags (planned) — emotional weighting that modulates consolidation and retrieval priority.
Perceptual buffers (planned) — ultra-short multimodal traces feed the event stream; priming via residual activation.
